setting.note: Expelled from home for his sexuality, the young A-Qing joins the hidden community of gay youths who gather after dark in Taipei's New Park, a secret 'kingdom of the night' with its own laws and legends. Pai maps the real city of the early 1970s: New Park with its lotus pond, the bar the outcasts open together, the family homes and military-dependents' villages they are cast out of, and the neon nightlife of Ximending.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Pai Hsien-yung, first published 1983 (Niezi). Mapped as a cited work: no text stored, no passages reproduced. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: Crystal Boys, Pai Hsien-yung (1983). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The violet of the night, the colour of the hidden 'dark kingdom' in the park where the outcast boys make a world of their own. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
